overview: This Mono-Red Aggro deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly by deploying cheap, aggressive creatures and leveraging combat damage triggers. Signature cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Screaming Nemesis provide early pressure and board control, while Eidolon of the Great Revel serves as the primary win condition by multiplying damage output. Success relies on maintaining relentless tempo and closing the game before opponents can stabilize.
win_conditions: Direct combat damage from early creatures
Repeatedly triggering Eidolon of the Great Revel to drain life totals
Using Screaming Nemesis to clear blockers or deal direct damage

keep_criteria: Hands with at least one turn-one creature
Hands containing Eidolon of the Great Revel with early triggers
Hands with Swiftspear paired with instant-speed removal
Keep hands with 1–2 lands and 2–3 one-drops (Screaming Nemesis, Eidolon of the Great Revel, Lightning Strike, Firebrand, etc.).
Prioritize hands that can play a turn-1 one-drop or turn-2 Monastery Swiftspear.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with only three-mana or higher cards
Hands lacking early creatures or damage triggers
Hands with multiple Eidolons or no lands
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands, no early threats, or only high-cost spells.

early_game: Play lands and Monastery Swiftspear immediately to establish pressure
Cast Screaming Nemesis to remove early threats or deal damage
Prioritize keeping the initiative and forcing opponent responses
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a one-drop if possible.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast a second one-drop or Monastery Swiftspear.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast early threats and have mana available for pump spells or burn.
Avoid overcommitting to the board if you suspect a sweeper.
mid_game: Trigger Eidolon of the Great Revel multiple times per turn using combat and spells
Trade efficiently to maintain board presence while pushing damage
Use removal to clear path for aggressive attacks
Treat mana as a resource for threats and burn, not for storing.
Use discard to disrupt opponent’s hand on their turn, preferably targeting key spells or combo pieces.
The graveyard is generally irrelevant for this deck, but monitor opponent’s graveyard for recursion or mill strategies.
Commit threats when the opponent has no sweepers or cannot afford to block.
Hold back if they have potential board wipes or heavy removal.
Use direct damage to clear blockers, finish players, or punish draw-go strategies.
late_game: Rely on accumulated Eidolon triggers to close out the match
Accept that the deck falls off if the opponent stabilizes
Push all remaining damage through before opponent's card advantage takes over
Monastery Swiftspear (pumped via combat tricks or spells), direct damage spells, and coordinated combat with multiple threats.
Win by applying early pressure, using discard to clear the path, and burning the opponent down to lethal.
Go all-in when the opponent has no sweepers, has low life total, or you have enough threats to trade favorably.
Commit when you can force unfavorable blocks or when you can finish the game before they can respond.

key_cards.name: Monastery Swiftspear
key_cards.notes: Deploy on turn one to force early answers and establish a threatening board state.

key_cards.name: Screaming Nemesis
key_cards.notes: Use to remove key blockers or deal direct damage when the board is clear, maximizing tempo.

key_cards.name: Eidolon of the Great Revel
key_cards.notes: Cast when you can trigger it multiple times in a turn to rapidly reduce opponent's life total.

sequencing_tips: Hold Screaming Nemesis until the opponent's turn to protect your board or answer threats
Trigger Eidolon of the Great Revel after combat damage is dealt to maximize life loss
Play Swiftspear before casting other spells to ensure early pressure and force responses
Common mistakes include overextending into sweepers, misusing discard on irrelevant cards, and failing to track opponent’s mana for counterspells or sweepers.
Unique interactions: Monastery Swiftspear’s double strike makes it a premier finisher
Screaming Nemesis punishes draw-go and control strategies
Eidolon of the Great Revel pressures spell-heavy decks.
Always prioritize discarding key spells over threats, and use burn to clear blockers or finish players efficiently.

matchups.advice: Push damage aggressively before they can assemble counterspells and board wipes
Race them by triggering Eidolon repeatedly and avoiding unfavorable trades
Use Nemesis to trade efficiently and protect your life total while racing

Use discard to remove counterspells before they can be cast. Cast threats in response to their mana sources or use multiple threats to force counter-decisions. If possible, play around counters by holding up mana for pump spells or burn.
Apply maximum pressure early to prevent them from reaching their ramp spells. Use discard to hit their ramp or mana rocks. Don’t let them stabilize; race them or disrupt their early game.
Win the discard war. Use more discard and direct damage to disrupt their hand. Track opponent’s hand and play accordingly. Prioritize threats that are hard to remove and use burn to finish the game.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Cyclonic Rift), counters, and discard outlets.
Key tells include holding up mana on turn 2–3, drawing extra cards, or playing unusual lands.
Track opponent’s life total and hand size to optimize discard and burn timing.
